title: 怪屋
summary:
Charles informs Taverner and the father that Roger Leonides was planning to flee abroad following a major argument with his father on the day the old man died. Based on Josephine's testimony, the group considers whether Roger embezzled company funds. Taverner suggests a theory where Roger or his wife, Clementine, switched the old man's insulin with a high concentration of isoleucin. Charles expresses a belief that Clementine is the likely perpetrator, noting her calm and capable nature, and suggests she might have acted ruthlessly to protect her husband.

The following day, Charles meets with his father and Taverner at Scotland Yard. Taverner reveals that while Roger has not committed fraud, his poor business judgment has left the family catering company on the verge of bankruptcy. To save the company, Roger needs a massive sum of money by the following Wednesday, which he could potentially secure by using his father's will to apply for a loan. It is deduced that Roger may have killed his father to ensure he could inherit the funds necessary to prevent insolvency, especially after Aristides Leonides likely refused to invest in the failing business.

The father prepares to interrogate Roger, even hiring a stenographer for the session. When Roger Leonides arrives, appearing clumsy and hurried, Charles observes his demeanor and concludes that while Roger may be involved, Clementine is the one who actually executed the poisoning. Roger enters the room talking incessantly, asking for news regarding the investigation.
